language.name: English
app.title: Vektorrazor - PNG logo to CAD-ready vector data
nav.scale_export: Scale and export DXF / SVG / STL
step1.label: Step 1 of 2: edit image / prepare exact colors
step2.label: Step 2 of 2: vectorize / export DXF or SVG
status.auto_from_image_done: Auto settings based on image applied.
status.auto_from_image_textlogo: Auto settings: text/logo mode (fine details prioritized).
status.auto_expert_done: Expert values automatically set from image.
status.startup_preset_logo: Startup preset applied: Logo / CAD / clear shapes.
status.workflow_reset: Workflow reset. Please load a new image.
language.incomplete: Language file incomplete, fallback used for missing texts.
step1.update_hint: Note: 'Continue to vectorizing' imports the current preview and switches to step 2. 'Use preview as new base' stays in step 1 and turns the preview into the new source image.
step1.basic_hint: Tip: Step 1 writes exact RGB colors into the intermediate PNG. These RGB values are automatically used as layer rules in step 2.
step1.manual_status: Short-click the original image to copy the color into the selected row. Dragging pans the preview.
step1.logo_hint: For gray logos, shadows, or gradients: create a mask from local contrast.
step1.logo_radius_hint: larger = shadows/gradients are more likely ignored
step1.photo_scan_hint: Technical auto mode for difficult photos and scans: detects paper/background, calculates several variants, reduces specks, protects fine lines, and creates a few clean target RGB colors. No AI, no training, no model dependency.
step1.problem_hint_body:
For difficult images, manual color selection is often better:
choose the main colors, use high tolerance, and replace them with clear technical colors. This collects color variation and noise more reliably and creates calmer areas for later vectorization.
step2.actions_hint: Auto values are optional and calculate a preview. Then check the preview or export directly.
step2.scale_export_title: Scale and export DXF / SVG / STL
step2.selection_help: Selection mode ON: click = select path, CTRL+click = add/toggle, ALT+click = remove directly. Selection mode OFF: click/drag pans the preview; only CTRL+click selects temporarily.
tooltip.step1.black_point: Less = more shadows. More = dark areas become black sooner.
tooltip.step1.white_point: Less = bright areas become white sooner. More = more bright detail.
tooltip.step1.gamma: Less = brighter midtones. More = darker midtones.
tooltip.step1.rotation: Rotates the intermediate image. This rotation is used for step 2 and export.
tooltip.step1.threshold: Less = stricter color split. More = merge similar colors.
tooltip.step1.min_area: Less = keep tiny details. More = ignore small noise.
tooltip.step1.max_colors: Less = few main colors. More = more color shades.
tooltip.step1.alpha_from: Less = count more transparent pixels. More = ignore transparency more.
tooltip.step1.noise_suppression: Less = more detail. More = remove more noise.
tooltip.step1.logo_threshold: Less = more becomes logo. More = only stronger strokes.
tooltip.step1.logo_radius: Less = local detail. More = ignore shadows/gradients more.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_max_colors: Less = coarse color groups. More = more subject colors.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_min_area: Less = keep fine parts. More = remove small specks.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_noise: Less = keep more texture. More = clean noise harder.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_foreground_dist: Less = weak colors become subject sooner. More = stricter background split.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_weak_contrast: More = recover low-contrast fine details, weak text, and thin lines more strongly.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_protect_backgro: Off = normal. On = lock paper/background so it does not become a target color.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_object_mask_fir: On = detect likely subject areas first. Helps with paper texture and blotchy backgrounds.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_despeckle: Off = keep mini islands. On = remove very small foreign specks per color mask.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_despeckle_area: Less = keep more small details. More = remove small speck islands more strongly.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_close_lines: On = carefully close small gaps in lines and circles.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_fill_small_hole: On = reduce small holes inside real filled areas.
tooltip.step2.pixel_to_mm: Less = smaller export. More = larger export.
tooltip.step2.merge_lines: Less = keep lines separate. More = connect nearby lines.
tooltip.step2.global_epsilon: Maximum allowed deviation in pixels. Less = more points/detail. More = fewer points/more CAD-friendly.
tooltip.step2.cad_deviation: Douglas-Peucker deviation in pixels. 0.10 very accurate, 0.80 calmer, 3.00 strongly simplified.
tooltip.step2.scale_export_tolerance_per: Less = more accurate and more points. More = stronger simplification relative to image size.
tooltip.step2.anchor_min_distance: Removes neighboring points from the same path if they are closer than this distance. Other paths are not touched.
tooltip.step2.dedupe_tolerance: Less = stricter. More = remove similar duplicate lines.
tooltip.step2.preprocess_blur: Less = sharper. More = calmer pixel edges.
tooltip.step2.preprocess_edges: Less = closer to raster. More = calmer edges.
tooltip.step2.preprocess_noise: Less = keep tiny detail. More = delete small noise.
tooltip.step2.internal_scale: Less = faster. More = finer detection, slower.
tooltip.step2.smart_corner_angle: Less = smooth more. More = protect more corners.
tooltip.step2.smart_line_tolerance: Less = stricter straight lines. More = treat more lines as straight.
tooltip.step2.smart_curve_strength: Less = more original. More = calmer curves.
tooltip.step2.hole_scale: Less = smaller inner holes. More = larger inner holes.
tooltip.step2.bridge_width_mm: Less = narrow bridges. More = wider bridges.
tooltip.step2.bridge_width_percent: Less = fixed mm matters more. More = width follows image size.
tooltip.step2.bridge_count: Less = fewer interruptions. More = parts are held more securely.
tooltip.step2.delete_small: Off = delete nothing. mm/% = filter small contours.
tooltip.step2.delete_small_mm: Less = keep more small parts. More = delete more small parts.
tooltip.step2.delete_small_percent: Less = more detail. More = keep only larger objects.
tooltip.step2.zoom: Less = zoom out. More = zoom in.
msg.no_intermediate_step: Please import step 1 first or load a PNG directly.
msg.accepted: The edited image was imported for step 2.
msg.welcome_body:
Vektorrazor works in two steps:

1. Prepare image
Load an image and adjust colors if needed so the contrast is clear.
When loading an image, Auto mode suggests suitable values. If you want to adjust things yourself, choose No.

2. Vectorize
When the intermediate image looks right, continue to step 2.
There Vektorrazor detects the shapes and creates the preview and export.
msg.step1_auto_prompt_title: Use auto mode for this image?
msg.step1_auto_prompt_body:
Should Vektorrazor analyze the image after loading and suggest suitable values?

Yes: auto detection starts after loading.
No: only load the image; you adjust values yourself.
msg.step1_recommend_existing_bw:
This image already has strong contrast and works as a black/white source.

Use the existing image unchanged?

Yes: keep it 1:1 as the intermediate image and use black/white rules in step 2.
No: open manual editing.
status.step1_auto_skipped: Image loaded. Auto detection was skipped.
msg.perfect_bw_body:
This image is already a clean black/white image.

It is kept 1:1. When switching to step 2, Vektorrazor automatically uses black/white rules.
startup_preset.choose: Which source type fits best? You can change this anytime later in step 2.
startup_preset.logo.title: Logo / CAD / clear shapes
startup_preset.logo.desc: Clear edges, calmer straight lines, fewer points and Smart CAD Smoothing more likely enabled.
startup_preset.organic.title: Image / organic
startup_preset.organic.desc: Preserve more detail, avoid hard straightening and treat organic contours carefully.
startup_preset.mixed.title: Mixed
startup_preset.mixed.desc: Balanced preset between clear shapes and organic subjects.
msg.auto_expert_prompt:
Should Vektorrazor suggest suitable expert values for this image and apply them now?

Yes: values are set automatically and the preview is recalculated.
No: current values stay unchanged.
msg.auto_expert_prompt_details:
Smart CAD Smoothing keeps straight areas (for example building edges) straighter, smooths rounded areas (for example eyes/curves), and keeps organic shapes (for example tree trunks) more natural.
Edge calming helps with pixelated source images so lines are detected cleaner and straighter.
msg.motif_recalculate_body:
Should Vektorrazor recalculate the auto values using the new source type?

Yes: Auto check runs again and the preview is updated.
No: Only the profile is set; you can keep adjusting values manually.
msg.preprocess_info_body:
Preprocessing changes only the working image used for vector detection, not the original image.

Blur: Higher values calm pixel edges more strongly, but can remove fine detail. Lower values preserve detail, but keep more stair-stepping.
Edge calming: This belongs to preprocessing. Higher values smooth pixelated contours more strongly so lines become straighter and more CAD-friendly. Lower values stay closer to the raster image.
Minimum noise px: Higher values remove more small specks and dots. Lower values preserve more tiny details.
Internal scaling: Higher values run detection at a finer working size and can clean curves, but are slower.
msg.smart_smoothing_info_body:
Smart CAD Smoothing analyzes the detected vector contours, after preprocessing.

Straight areas such as building edges stay straighter. Round areas such as eyes, flowers, or ornaments are smoothed. Organic shapes such as tree trunks should not become perfectly technical, but keep their natural irregularity.

Protect corners deg: Higher values protect more angles as corners, lower values smooth more transitions.
Straight line tolerance px: Higher values classify more regions as straight lines, lower values are stricter.
Curve smoothing: Higher values make curves rounder and calmer, lower values preserve more original irregularity.
msg.bridge_tabs_info_body:
Bridges open closed risk contours at short positions. This keeps inner islands and small cutout parts connected to the material so they are less likely to fall out while cutting.

Bridge width mm: Higher values create wider bridges, lower values keep more of the original contour.
Bridge width % of image: Alternative width based on image size. If mm and percent are set, the larger value is used.
Bridges per part: More bridges hold parts more securely, but add more interruptions to the contour.

The Cut/dropout risk preview shows which parts would be critical without bridges. Enabled bridges are exported to SVG/DXF.
msg.no_intermediate_load_first: Please load or edit an image first.
msg.no_bbox_body: The current contour has no valid size.
msg.target_size_body: Enter a target width or target height in mm.
msg.profile_apply_body: This only applies the selected profile and replaces the color/layer rules. Changes to individual colors are recalculated with 'Update preview'.
msg.lineart_recommend_intro:
This intermediate image looks like black/white line art. Detail-friendly values are recommended: no automatic blur, no small-object deletion, and black/white rules from the intermediate PNG.

Choose which preview should open now:
msg.lineart_choice_contour: shows the actual vector paths and is usually best for CAD checking
msg.lineart_choice_mask: shows raw raster color detection before contour finding
msg.lineart_expert_hint: You can change this later in Expert mode under 'Preview view'.
msg.step1_recommend_mask:
This image looks like a bright/gray logo on a bright or gradient background.

Recommendation: create a logo mask.
The values were derived from the image, not fixed defaults.

Suggestion:
- Logo threshold: {threshold}
- Background radius: {blur}
- estimated mask area: about {coverage:.1f}%
- target range from analysis: about {target:.1f}%

Apply now?

No = stay on the matching tab only, values are not changed.
msg.step1_recommend_bw:
This image looks like high-contrast black/white line art.

Recommendation: create a black/white mask instead of carrying many residual edge colors into vectorization.
Suggestion:
- Logo threshold: {threshold}
- Background radius: {blur}
- fine details are preserved without extra pixel cleanup
- then check contour lines in step 2

Apply now?

No = stay on the matching tab only, values are not changed.
msg.step1_recommend_manual:
This image does not match a clear automatic mode.

Recommendation: manual color mapping.
You can click a color in the original image and assign the target RGB directly.

The matching tab has already been opened.
msg.busy_load_image_body:
Image is being loaded and analyzed...

Please wait a moment. Clicks are blocked during this process.
msg.busy_detect_colors_body:
Colors and areas are being analyzed...

Please wait a moment.
msg.busy_vector_body:
Contours and preview are being calculated.

Clicks in the main window are blocked while this is running.
status.epsilon_applied_all: CAD deviation applied to all colors: {value}px
status.scale_calculated: Scale set: {pixel_to_mm} mm/px. CAD deviation equals {tolerance_px}px.
status.perfect_bw_ready: Perfect black/white image detected: kept 1:1, step 2 uses black/white rules.
status.existing_image_used: Existing image kept unchanged. Step 2 uses matching black/white rules.
status.existing_image_skipped: Existing image was not applied automatically. Manual editing opened.
status.step1_recommend_mask_applied: Recommendation applied: logo mask with threshold {threshold}, radius {blur}.
status.step1_recommend_mask_skipped: Recommendation: logo mask. Values were not changed.
status.step1_recommend_bw_skipped: Recommendation: black/white mask. Values were not changed.
status.step1_recommend_color_skipped: Recommendation: reduce colors. Values were not changed.
status.step1_recommend_photo_scan_applie: Recommendation applied: photo/scan cleanup with problem score {score}.
status.step1_recommend_photo_scan_skippe: Recommendation: photo/scan cleanup with problem score {score}. Values were not changed.
status.photo_scan_done: Photo/scan cleanup created: {variant}, {count} target colors, problem score {score}, noise {noise:.1f}, technical score {tech:.1f}.
status.problem_hint_shown: Hint: For this difficult image, manual color selection with higher tolerance may create better areas.
status.problem_hint_manual_opened: Manual color selection opened. Click the main colors in the original image.
status.problem_hint_high_tolerance: High tolerance set for manual color mapping.
msg.cad_cleanup_needs_redetect: Raw contours are missing for this window. Please refresh vectorization once.
msg.photo_scan_timeout:
A photo/scan mask step took too long and was cancelled.
Try fewer target colors, stronger noise cleanup, or a lower weak-detail setting.
status.step1_recommend_manual: No clear automatic mode detected: manual color mapping recommended.
status.detected_color_regions: {count} color regions detected. Target RGB can be adjusted directly in the table.
status.manual_row_selected: Selected row: #{row} | click in original image copies the color.
status.logo_mask_created: Logo mask created. This mask can be vectorized directly in step 2.
status.vector_source_ready_transferred: Edited image is ready for step 2. Color/layer rules were imported from the intermediate PNG.
status.vector_source_ready_autofill: Edited image is ready for step 2. Color/layer rules were suggested automatically.
status.path_selection_changed: Path selection changed. Del or the button removes selected paths.
status.selection_mode_on: Selection mode active: click in vector preview selects a path. CTRL adds, ALT removes directly.
status.selection_mode_off: Selection mode off: preview can be panned normally.
status.path_selected_details: Selected: path #{index} | layer {layer} | points {points} | area about {area:.0f}px²
status.paths_selected_count: {count} paths selected | Del or button removes all selected paths
status.path_removed: Path removed. Remaining: {remaining} | Export active: {exported} | Points: {points}
step2.stl_select_hint: Click the preview to toggle outer surfaces or inner holes. Purple outer surfaces are extruded. Red inner holes are cut out and therefore NOT extruded. Orange inner holes are filled/extruded. CTRL is not required.
msg.stl_no_surfaces_body: STL export needs closed exportable surfaces. Please generate a suitable preview first.
msg.stl_no_selection_body: Please select at least one surface for STL export.
msg.stl_invalid_extrusion: Enter an extrusion value greater than 0 mm.
step2.scale_default_hint: Default: 1 px = 1 mm. A 500 × 500 px image is exported as 500 × 500 mm without scaling.
step2.stl_selection_legend: Legend: purple = extruded | gray = outer surface not extruded | red with cross = hole is cut out/not extruded | orange with cross = hole is filled/extruded.
step2.stl_preview_note: STL preview: red holes are actually cut out. If this option is disabled, inner holes are filled/extruded.
step1.methods_intro:
Each working mode is a separate way to create the technical intermediate image.
You can use the current preview below as a new base and then continue in another mode.
status.photo_scan_mode_auto: Auto mode: multiple technical variants will be scored.
status.photo_scan_mode_clean: Cleaner mode: stronger smoothing and speck removal.
status.photo_scan_mode_detail: Detail mode: fine lines and weak details are protected more strongly.
status.photo_scan_mode_color: Color mode: several main colors are preserved.
status.photo_scan_mode_bw: Black/white mode: adaptive light/dark mask for line art and text.
status.preview_base_ready: Current preview applied as new base image.
msg.preview_base_body: The current preview is now the new base image in step 1. You can continue editing in another tab.
step1.preprocess_hint: This page only prepares the image: brightness, contrast, levels, gamma, and rotation. Automatic color detection is now in its own tab, “Basic: detect colors”. Use “Use preview as new base” to make the prepared preview the new working base for the other tabs.
status.photo_scan_mode_faded: Faded print mode: paper/lighting variation is removed and weak print traces are extracted as a black/white mask.
step1.mode_hint.prep:
Prepare brightness, contrast, levels, gamma, and rotation.
Afterwards you can use the preview as a new base or continue in another mode.
step1.mode_hint.basic:
Automatic color detection for already fairly clear logos.
Similar colors are collected and converted into exact technical RGB target colors.
step1.mode_hint.manual:
Manual color selection with picker and custom tolerance.
Useful when you know exactly which main colors should be kept or replaced.
step1.mode_hint.logo:
Local contrast mask for gray logos, shadows, or slight gradients.
Useful for simple logos that need to be separated from the background.
step1.mode_hint.photo_scan:
Technical auto mode for difficult photos and scans.
Calm paper/background, remove noise, find weak details, and create a few clear target colors.
tooltip.step1.photo_scan_preserve_accent: Keeps small colored details such as stars. The target color is chosen locally: white on dark surroundings, black on light surroundings.
status.step1_recommend_photo_scan_prefer: For logo/badge: photo/scan black/white is preferred over recoloring colors.
